entities.name: RAIN-FED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agriculture that relies solely on rainfall for water supply

entities.name: ARID OR SEMI ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Climatic regions characterized by low and erratic rainfall

entities.name: KOTHAPALLY
entities.description: A micro watershed of 450 ha area in Southern India

entities.name: OSMAN SAGAR
entities.description: A reservoir located in the Musi catchment

entities.name: MUSI CATCHMENT
entities.description: The drainage basin of the Musi river in Southern India

entities.name: SOUTHERN INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the study was conducted

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Precipitation that is highly erratic in amount and distribution in the region

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Center for Research in Semi-Arid Tropics, a lead organization in the consortium

entities.name: CRIDA
entities.description: Central Research Institute for Dryland Agriculture, a national partner

entities.name: NRSA
entities.description: National Remote Sensing Agency, a national partner

entities.name: DWMA
entities.description: District Water Management Agency, a national partner

entities.name: HYDERABAD
entities.description: City in Andhra Pradesh where partners are located

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: State in India where the study area is located

entities.name: COMMUNITY BASED WATERSHED DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM
entities.description: A program started in 1999 to develop the Kothapally watershed

entities.name: WATER FLOWS
entities.description: The movement and volume of water within the watershed

entities.name: CROP PARAMETERS
entities.description: Measurable characteristics of crops in the area

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production per unit area

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER ASSESSMENT TOOL (SWAT)
entities.description: A GIS-based hydrological model used to study water balance

entities.name: WATER BALANCE
entities.description: The equilibrium between water input and output in the watershed system

entities.name: WATER MANAGEMENT OPTIONS
entities.description: Different approaches used to manage water resources

entities.name: FLOW REDUCTION
entities.description: The decrease in the amount of water flowing out of the system

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The amount of soil eroded from the fields

entities.name: CHECK-DAMS
entities.description: Structures built to slow water flow and increase groundwater recharge

entities.name: GROUNDWATER RECHARGE
entities.description: The process of water infiltrating into the underground aquifers

entities.name: SUPPLEMENTARY IRRIGATION
entities.description: Additional water provided to crops when rainfall is insufficient

entities.name: MONSOON CROP
entities.description: Crops grown during the monsoon season

entities.name: SECOND CROP
entities.description: Crops grown after the monsoon season

entities.name: IN-SITU SOIL WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Techniques to conserve water within the soil profile

entities.name: OUTFLOW
entities.description: The volume of water leaving the watershed system

entities.name: EVAPOTRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The sum of evaporation from the land surface and transpiration from plants

entities.name: SURFACE RUNOFF
entities.description: Water that flows over the land surface rather than infiltrating

entities.name: GROUNDWATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of groundwater accessible for use

entities.name: ET FLOW
entities.description: The flow of water lost through evapotranspiration

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of the agricultural system to withstand and recover from stress

entities.name: LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of securing the necessities of life for the farmers
